source_quote: date
CHW name
child's name
age (years and months)
sex
caregiver's name
relationship (mother/father/other)
address
ASK the caregiver about the child's problems

display_name: Does the child have a cough?
source_quote: Does the child have a cough?
If yes, for how long? A cough for 14 days or more is a danger sign.

display_name: Does the child have fever now or in the last 3 days?
source_quote: Does the child have fever now or in the last 3 days?
If yes, when did it start?
Fever for 7 days or more is a danger sign.

display_name: Does the child have diarrhoea (≥3 loose/watery stools in 24 hrs)?
source_quote: Diarrhoea is 3 or more loose or watery stools in 24 hours.
Ask: For how long? ... Diarrhoea for 14 days or more is a danger sign.

display_name: Is there blood in the stool?
source_quote: Is there blood in the stool? ... Blood in stool (dysentery) is a danger sign.

display_name: Has the child had a convulsion during this illness?
source_quote: A convulsion during the current illness is a danger sign. The child needs urgent referral.

display_name: Is the child not able to drink or feed anything?
source_quote: A child who cannot drink or feed anything at all has a danger sign. Ask the caregiver to offer the child a drink or breastfeed.
A child who vomits everything and cannot hold anything down has a danger sign.

display_name: Is the child unusually sleepy, difficult to wake, or unconscious?
source_quote: A child who is unusually sleepy, difficult to wake, or unconscious has a danger sign. Try to wake the child by clapping or speaking loudly.

display_name: Does the child have chest indrawing?
source_quote: Look at the lower chest wall when the child breathes IN. Chest indrawing is when the lower chest wall goes in.
Count the child's breaths for one full minute while the child is calm and quiet.
Use the MUAC (Mid-Upper Arm Circumference) strap on children age 6 months up to 5 years. Wrap the strap around the child's left upper arm, midway between shoulder and elbow.

display_name: Does the child have bilateral pitting oedema of both feet?
source_quote: Also check for swelling of both feet (oedema). Press thumbs gently on the top of each foot for 3 seconds. If dents remain on BOTH feet, the child has severe malnutrition.
POSITIVE = red lines in both C and T windows. NEGATIVE = red line in C window only. INVALID = no line in C window; repeat with new test.

display_name: Fever identified by feeling child's stomach or underarm
source_quote: Identify fever by the caregiver's report or by feeling the child's stomach or underarm.

display_name: At 3-day follow-up: Is the child still having diarrhoea?
source_quote: After 3 days: Is the child still having diarrhoea? If yes and for less than 14 days, continue ORS and zinc. If 14 days or more, refer.

display_name: At 3-day follow-up: Does the child still have fever?
source_quote: After 3 days: Does the child still have fever? If RDT was positive and antimalarial was given and still has fever, refer.

display_name: At 3-day follow-up: Is the child still coughing?
source_quote: After 3 days: Is the child still coughing? Count breaths again. If still fast breathing, refer. If breathing normal, complete amoxicillin course.

display_name: Respiratory rate at 3-day follow-up (breaths per minute)
source_quote: Count breaths again. If still fast breathing, refer.

display_name: General signs of illness observed on LOOK assessment
source_quote: LOOK for signs of illness
